ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Chilli Cheese Toast; mapped to generic toast food concept as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for chilli cheese toast; A0FKP (toasted bread) used as the closest defensible base category. Cheese and chilli toppings are not separately coded.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for chilli cheese toast as a composite dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Chilli Cheese Toast is approximately 80 g, equivalent to one to two slices of bread topped with cheese and chilli, as commonly served at Indian-style cafes and kopitiams in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Chilli Cheese Toast — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Chilli Cheese Toast (cr_id G00010756). All per-100g values are sourced from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — white bread GI reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Chilli Cheese Toast as a composite dish. GI estimated at 70 (high) based on the white bread component, which is the primary carbohydrate source. White bread GI is consistently reported at 70–75 in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). The cheese and chilli toppings contribute negligible carbohydrate and do not materially alter the GI. Glycemic load per 80 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (70 × 7.08) / 100 ≈ 5.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Extremely high sodium content at 1279 mg per 100 g
hypertension.guidance: Chilli Cheese Toast is contraindicated for individuals with hypertension; a single 80 g serving delivers approximately 1023 mg sodium — more than half the 2000 mg daily recommended limit — primarily from processed cheese and bread, and should be strictly avoided or replaced with a low-sodium cheese on wholegrain bread.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI white bread base with zero dietary fibre and 6.24 g sugar per 100 g
type2_diabetes.guidance: The white bread base has a GI of approximately 70 and provides zero dietary fibre, causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this dish or substitute wholegrain high-fibre bread and limit portion size to minimise glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~12.5 g/100 g), trans fat (0.1 g/100 g), and very high sodium (1279 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of high saturated fat from processed cheese, trace trans fat from processed ingredients, and critically elevated sodium makes Chilli Cheese Toast a high cardiovascular risk food; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption and opt for low-fat cheese on wholegrain bread with no added salt.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat (~12.5 g/100 g) and trace trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) from processed cheese are pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The high saturated fat content from processed cheese and trace trans fat from processed ingredients are strong pro-inflammatory triggers for rheumatoid arthritis; green chilli is a nightshade ingredient that some individuals with RA may need to monitor, and this dish should be avoided or substantially modified.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chilli Cheese Toast is a popular Indian-origin snack and light meal widely consumed in Singapore, particularly at Indian-style cafes, kopitiams, and hawker stalls. It consists of sliced white bread — typically sandwich loaf — generously topped with a mixture of processed cheese (such as cheddar or processed cheese slices), finely chopped green chillies, and sometimes butter or margarine, then toasted or grilled until the cheese melts and the bread is golden and crisp. The result is a rich, savoury, mildly spicy toast with a creamy, slightly stretchy cheese layer and a crunchy bread base. The flavour profile is dominated by the saltiness of the processed cheese, the mild heat of the green chilli, and the buttery richness of the toasted bread. Nutritionally, Chilli Cheese Toast is energy-dense at 307 kcal per 100 g, with a high fat content of 23.06 g (predominantly saturated fat estimated at ~12.5 g from processed cheese) and a moderate protein content of 16.09 g. Carbohydrate content is relatively low at 8.85 g per 100 g due to the high cheese-to-bread ratio, but the white bread base carries a high glycaemic index of approximately 70 with zero dietary fibre, making it unsuitable for blood sugar management. The sodium content is critically high at 1279 mg per 100 g, driven by processed cheese and bread, posing significant risk for hypertension and cardiovascular disease. Calcium is notably high at 1375 mg per 100 g, reflecting the substantial cheese content. This dish is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease without significant modification.
common_user_queries: Is chilli cheese toast good for diabetics?
Can I eat chilli cheese toast with high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for Chilli Cheese Toast (cr_id G00010756) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore dishes. Saturated fat and trans fat are estimated from known composition of processed cheese and butter components, documented in notes. GI is an analogous estimate based on white bread GI from the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: 1. Saturated fat estimated at ~12.5 g/100 g based on typical processed cheese (cheddar-type) composition per USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy, Cheese, cheddar, FDC ID 173414); this value is not directly reported in the HPB source. 2. Trans fat estimated at 0.1 g/100 g from processed cheese and margarine components; not directly reported in HPB source. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not available in the HPB Foodo database entry for this dish as of 2026-04-16. 4. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the HPB source for this dish. 5. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail beyond the HPB-reported fields not available in the HPB Foodo source for this composite dish as of 2026-04-16. 6. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB source; white bread base with processed cheese topping provides negligible dietary fibre. 7. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on white bread GI from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for Chilli Cheese Toast as a composite dish was found in approved sources. 8. Halal status assumed based on typical ingredients (bread, cheese, chilli, butter) with no pork or alcohol; individual product verification recommended.
